zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Scrope於大約1336年出生時，她的父親Sir Henry Scrope25歲，母親Joan21歲。她於1350年9月與Hugh FitzHugh在英國越可爾拉文斯沃斯結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1386年在英國越可爾拉文斯沃斯享年51歲。

nameMeanings.name: Scrope
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman: of uncertain origin. Tengvik suggests derivation from the Old Scandinavian personal name Skrópi (of doubtful etymology, but compare Old Scandinavian skrópa-maðr ‘hypocrite’, containing the genitive plural form of skrópar ‘sham, hypocrisy’). It is possible, but not certain, that this personal name forms the first element of the Notts place-name Scrooby. Otherwise there is no evidence for its currency in England or, more relevantly, in Normandy. Richards Castle (in Herefs and Shrops) was apparently named after Richard Scrope, who held estates in Herefs and Worcs in 1066 and was a Norman favourite of Edward the Confessor. The Scrope family, whose name is traditionally pronounced /skru:p/, was politically important in medieval England, and acquired estates in Lincs (Barton on Humber), NR Yorks (Castle Bolton), and in ER Yorks (Flotmanby). Richard (le) Scrope of Bolton, in Wensleydale, was Lord Chancellor in the reign of Richard II. The use of le and de in the surname, suggesting either a nickname or a locative name, must be treated with caution because these were often added gratuitously to Anglo-Norman surnames as a sign of high social status.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Scrope was born about 1336, her father, Sir Henry Scrope, was 25 and her mother, Joan, was 21. She married Hugh FitzHugh in September 1350, in Ravensworth, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1386, in Ravensworth, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 51.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Scrope est née environ 1336, son père, Sir Henry Scrope, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Joan, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Hugh FitzHugh en septembre 1350, à Ravensworth, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1386, à Ravensworth, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 51 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Scrope um 1336 geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Henry Scrope 25 und ihre Mutter Joan 21. Sie hat im September 1350 in Ravensworth, Yorkshire, England Hugh FitzHugh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1386 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Ravensworth, Yorkshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Scrope è nata il circa 1336, sua padre, Sir Henry Scrope, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Joan, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Hugh FitzHugh nel settembre 1350, nel Ravensworth, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1386, nel Ravensworth, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 51 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Scropeが約1336年に生まれた時，父Sir Henry Scropeは25歳，母Joanは21歳であった。1350年9月にイギリスヨークシュアイアラベンスウオルスでHugh FitzHughと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1386年にイギリスヨークシュアイアラベンスウオルスで51歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Scrope(이)가 대략 1336년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Henry Scrope(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1350년 9월 영국 요르크실르 레이븐스워스에서 Hugh FitzHugh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1386년 영국 요르크실르 레이븐스워스에서 51세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Scrope nasceu aproximadamente 1336, seu pai, Sir Henry Scrope, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Joan, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Hugh FitzHugh em setembro de 1350, em Ravensworth, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1386, em Ravensworth, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 51 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Scrope родилась около 1336 г., её отцу по имени Sir Henry Scrope было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hugh FitzHugh сентябрь 1350 г. в местности равенсворт, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1386 г. в местности равенсворт, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 51 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Scrope nació aproximadamente 1336, su papá, Sir Henry Scrope, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Joan, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Hugh FitzHugh en septiembre de 1350, en Ravensworth, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1386, en Ravensworth, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 51 años.
